comparison_note: All comparisons use completed days: yesterday (2026-07-21) is the run_date. Today is excluded as it is an incomplete day. Cell C Recharges compares yesterday against same-day-last-month and same-day-two-months-ago, plus MTD-vs-prior-MTD (same day-of-month cutoff, so periods are equal length). NOTE: this was a manual, incremental refresh across three passes on 2026-07-22 (Active 1 rebuilt; Sales added; DIM Subscriber Alignment and Terminations rebuilt with the tenant filter and new threshold) — Cell C Recharges is the only section still carried over unchanged from the last full automated run (run_date 2026-07-19); the scheduled dda-daily-refresh task has not produced a newer set for it and should be checked separately.

flags: STALE — carried over from run_date 2026-07-19, not refreshed in this pass.

0 recharges recorded for 2026-07-19 — total outage safety net triggered. Likely cause: data pipeline lag, not a real zero-transaction day — VW_CELLC_RECHARGES currently has no rows past 2026-07-17 (max transaction date), i.e. 2 days behind the 2026-07-19 run_date. Needs data-team follow-up to confirm the load has simply not caught up yet.
2026-07-19 recharge count (0) is >20% below same-day-last-month (2026-06-19: 18,267) and same-day-two-months-ago (2026-05-19: 14,799) — consistent with the same data lag.
2026-07-19 revenue (R0) is >20% below same-day-last-month (R276,874) and same-day-two-months-ago (R224,339) — consistent with the same data lag.
Same-day last-month vs two-months-ago count: up 23.4% (18,267 vs 14,799) — exceeds 5% threshold.
Same-day last-month vs two-months-ago value: up 23.4% (R276,874 vs R224,339) — exceeds 5% threshold.

Current MTD window (2026-07-01 to 2026-07-19) is affected by the same load lag — the underlying data only extends to 2026-07-17, so current MTD count/value likely understate the true month-to-date figures by roughly 2 days' worth of transactions. Read the MTD comparisons above with that caveat; they did not individually cross the 10% threshold, but that may be masking the shortfall.

REVENUE_WHATSAPP_PURCHASES_VALUE/QUANTITY excluded from every sum above — MAX() ~1.7e19, orders of magnitude beyond every other revenue column, with an unresolved 'Unknown Policy!' on both columns per DESCRIBE TABLE. Needs data-team/Snowflake-admin follow-up before those two streams can be trusted or included.
Yesterday vs same day last month qty: down 70.7% (5,471 vs 18,692) — exceeds 20% threshold.
Yesterday vs same day last month value: down 71.2% (R62,106 vs R215,860) — exceeds 20% threshold.
Yesterday vs same day two months ago qty: down 74.0% (5,471 vs 21,023) — exceeds 20% threshold.
Yesterday vs same day two months ago value: down 74.6% (R62,106 vs R244,941) — exceeds 20% threshold.
Same-day last-month vs two-months-ago qty: down 11.1% (18,692 vs 21,023) — exceeds 5% threshold.
Same-day last-month vs two-months-ago value: down 11.9% (R215,860 vs R244,941) — exceeds 5% threshold.

Tenant breakdown (yesterday vs same day last month, value): uConnect: R211,711 → R55,310 (-73.9%); ME&YOU: R4,149 → R6,796 (+63.8%). This is a uConnect-specific drop, not an overall/ME&YOU issue — ME&YOU's small revenue base actually grew over the same window. MAX(TRANSACTION_DATE) confirms yesterday's data is not lagging (2026-07-21 is fully loaded, 4,533 rows), so this reads as a real business drop rather than a load-lag artifact — worth investigating directly rather than waiting for the next load.

Day 2-4 registration-activity cohort: down 63.2% vs trailing-7-day average (925 vs avg 2,514) — exceeds 60% drastic-drop threshold. Registration volume itself is also trending down over the same window (2026-07-14 to 2026-07-21: 3,972 → 4,630 new subs 2-4 days prior), so this may be a real activation slowdown rather than a data artifact — worth a follow-up look rather than dismissing as noise.

Spread of 596 across subscriber tables — MERGE (453,992) vs VW_ACTIVE (453,396) — exceeds the 15-record threshold. Much smaller than the previously-reported 12,114 spread, which was inflated by not filtering UCONNECT_MAY_MERGE to MASTER_TENANT = 'uConnect' (that filter is now applied, per 2026-07-22 request — ME&YOU rows excluded).
Duplicate ACCOUNT_NUMBER/ICCID check: 2 duplicated account numbers (4 rows) and 7 duplicated ICCIDs (40 rows) among active uConnect records — both well under the 100 threshold, no flag.

No red flag on the MTD-vs-threshold rule (18,639 MTD terminations vs a 10,171 threshold — avg of 18,253 and 1,088 + 500), but termination dates look batch-applied within a month rather than flowing evenly day-by-day: 2026-05-01 to 05-21 had only 1,088 uConnect terminations, yet the FULL month of May had 26,879 (almost all backdated into the second half of the month). A day-of-month-cutoff MTD comparison, which works fine for recharges/sales, may not be a reliable read on this metric. Worth confirming with the data team whether termination processing runs in batches before trusting this trend either way.
